senses.definition: used to describe someone or something that attracts a great deal of public attention

examples.x: As a celebrity, she is always in the public eye.
Being in the public eye can be overwhelming for some people.
He has been in the public eye ever since his breakthrough performance.
Politicians often live their lives in the public eye.
She was constantly in the public eye due to her philanthropic work.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who is very successful and widely recognized

examples.x: The company's new CEO is a big name in the financial world.
The conference attracted many big names in the tech industry.

senses.definition: someone or something that manages to attract a lot of attention and popularity for a short period of time

examples.x: That celebrity may be the flavor of the month in Hollywood right now, but it's hard to say whether they will have continued success in the future.
The fashion world is constantly introducing new trends, but most of them are just the flavor of the month and quickly fade away.
The new smartphone model is currently the flavor of the week among tech enthusiasts.

senses.definition: to experience a sudden rise to fame and glory

examples.x: The actor is hitting the big time with his new movie.
The band hit the big time after their song was played on the radio.

senses.definition: to become well-known or respected in a particular field or area through one's achievements or actions

examples.x: Angela worked hard to make a name for herself in the fashion industry.
He made a name for himself as an innovator in the tech industry.

senses.definition: to reach a great level of fame and success

examples.x: Michael Jordan made it big as a basketball player.
Sally always dreamed of making it big as a singer.

word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ ɑːnðə mˈæp

senses.definition: to make someone or something become renowned

examples.x: The company's advertising campaign is putting their new product on the map and generating a lot of interest.
The singer's latest album has put her on the map as a talented musician.

senses.definition: to prove someone's innocence or remove suspicion surrounding someone's reputation

examples.x: After being accused of theft, John worked hard to clear his name by providing evidence that proved his innocence.
The celebrity had been the subject of countless tabloid stories and rumors, but she continued to work on clearing her name by providing interviews and statements that addressed the false allegations.

senses.definition: something or someone that is currently the subject of a lot of gossip and discussion among the people in a particular community or social circle

examples.x: The recent election and its results have been the talk of the town, with people discussing the impact on the community and the country as a whole.
The school's upcoming dance has become the talk of the town, with students excitedly discussing their outfits and plans for the evening.

senses.definition: to take action or make a statement in order to avoid embarrassment, humiliation, or loss of respect

examples.x: The politician tried to save face after a scandal by apologizing and promising to make amends for any wrongdoing.
He apologized publicly to save face after his mistake.
She lied about quitting to save face, though she was actually fired.
In order to save face, he downplayed the severity of the failure.

senses.definition: someone or something that receives a lot of attention by the public

examples.x: The athlete remained in the spotlight after breaking the world record.
The singer is in the spotlight for her new album release.
The social media influencer is in the limelight for their latest viral post.

senses.definition: to undo or reverse something that has already been said or done, particularly when it is impossible to truly forget or ignore its impact or consequences

word_list.word: big fish in a small pond
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɪɡ fˈɪʃ ɪn ɐ smˈɔːl pˈɑːnd

senses.definition: someone who is important or influential within a small or limited group, but who may not be as significant or well-known in a larger context

examples.x: John was a big fish in a small pond at his old job, but he's struggling to keep up with the competition at his new company.
She enjoyed being a big fish in a small pond, where her contributions were valued and recognized, but she knew she would have to leave her comfort zone to achieve greater success.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv ðə wˈɜːld æt wˈʌnz fˈiːt

senses.definition: to be highly successful and popular

examples.x: As the CEO of the successful tech startup, Max has the world at his feet and is changing the industry with his innovative ideas
The talented musician had the world at her feet after winning the prestigious award, and she knew she had a bright future ahead of her.
